reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.312(a)(2)(i) requires unique user identification, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that each user or service has a unique identity. This can be enforced by using unique service accounts for each application or user, avoiding shared service accounts, and auditing user identities through RBAC policies. Kubernetes RBAC can be configured to ensure that service accounts are not shared and that user identities are properly logged and audited.
title: 164_312_a_2_i: 164.312(a)(2)(i) Unique user identification
